summary: The 'Sync - Release Notes & API Diffs' CI workflow fails repeatedly because the prepare job's 'git add -A' captures changes to protected agent instruction files (SKILL.md, TEMPLATE.md) and workflow files, which the safe-outputs supply-chain protection blocks when the agent tries to create a PR.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Cannot create pull request: patch modifies protected files (.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md, .github/workflows/update-release-notes.lock.yml, .github/workflows/update-release-notes.md)

stepsToReproduce: Trigger the 'Sync - Release Notes & API Diffs' workflow (manually or via push to main)
Observe the Prepare job running generate.sh and executing 'git add -A'
Observe the Polish agent attempting to create a PR via create-pull-request safe-output
Safe-output blocks the PR because the patch includes protected files
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: GitHub Actions, ubuntu-latest runner, agentic-workflows v0.71.5

repoLinks.description: First failing run from branch mattleibow-release-notes-manual-notes
Second follow-up failure run targeting PR #3645
Third follow-up failure run targeting PR #3645
Fourth follow-up failure run targeting PR #3645
Prior similar failure (closed not_planned) - same protected-files pattern, more files
Prior similar failure (closed not_planned) - security-audit skill protected files

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The workflow uses gh-aw v0.71.5 (seen in lock file metadata) and continues to fail with the same protected-files error.

analysis.summary: The prepare job runs 'git add -A' which captures ALL working-tree changes — including changes to agent instruction files (SKILL.md, TEMPLATE.md) and compiled workflow YAML (.lock.yml) that exist on the source branch or are produced by the generate scripts. The safe-outputs create-pull-request tool's supply-chain protection correctly blocks PRs containing those files. The workflow has no 'allowed-files' configuration and no 'protected-files: fallback-to-issue' setting, so each run fails hard instead of falling back gracefully. This is a recurring pattern — identical issues (#4202, #4266) occurred before.
analysis.rationale: This is clearly a CI build pipeline bug: the workflow is failing 5+ times with the same protected-file error, not a one-off transient failure. The root cause is in the prepare job's 'git add -A' at .github/workflows/update-release-notes.md line ~142, which is too broad — it captures protected files along with the legitimate documentation output. The issue began from a manual dispatch against branch 'mattleibow-release-notes-manual-notes' (which itself modifies SKILL.md and workflow files as PR #4334), but the subsequent 4 agent-job failures on the main-branch runs (targeting PR #3645) suggest that SKILL.md/TEMPLATE.md are appearing in the working tree of main-branch runs as well, possibly because those files are being generated/modified by the prepare pipeline or are present on the PR branch.

codeInvestigation.finding: The prepare job runs 'git add -A && git diff --cached --binary > prepare.patch' which captures every working-tree change — not just the expected documentation output. If any protected files (SKILL.md, TEMPLATE.md, .lock.yml) are modified or tracked as new files relative to HEAD, they end up in the patch.

The safe-outputs create-pull-request configuration has no 'allowed-files' list and no 'protected-files: fallback-to-issue' setting. Without these, the supply-chain protection blocks the PR entirely rather than creating a fallback issue or silently excluding protected paths.

The SKILL.md file is an agent instruction file (hand-authored, not generated). It is correctly a protected file. Its presence in the prepare patch from the 'mattleibow-release-notes-manual-notes' branch is explained by that branch containing a version of SKILL.md that differs from what was on that branch's HEAD — likely PR #4334 is modifying SKILL.md as part of a release-notes workflow improvement.

TEMPLATE.md is also listed as protected and appears in the patch for main-branch runs (the 4 comment failures). This suggests that either the generate scripts modify TEMPLATE.md as part of their output, or TEMPLATE.md is out of sync between the PR branch and main.

keySignals.text: Cannot create pull request: patch modifies protected files (.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md, .github/workflows/update-release-notes.lock.yml, .github/workflows/update-release-notes.md)
keySignals.interpretation: The prepare patch from branch 'mattleibow-release-notes-manual-notes' includes SKILL.md and the compiled workflow YAML, which are protected agent/workflow files. This indicates the source branch has modifications to those files (PR #4334).

keySignals.text: Cannot create pull request: patch modifies protected files (.agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/release-notes/references/TEMPLATE.md)
keySignals.source: issue comments — 4 subsequent agent-job failures targeting PR #3645
keySignals.interpretation: Even after the source switched to main-branch runs targeting PR #3645, SKILL.md and TEMPLATE.md are still appearing. This may indicate these files changed on main since the PR branch was created, causing a diff against the PR branch head.

The first failure was manually dispatched against a non-main branch that is the source branch for PR #4334, which is specifically a PR to modify the release-notes workflow/skill files — explaining why those protected files appear in the patch.

nextQuestions: Does PR #4334 modify .agents/skills/release-notes/SKILL.md and the workflow YAML files? If so, why was the workflow manually dispatched against that branch?
Why does TEMPLATE.md appear in main-branch runs (comment failures targeting PR #3645)? Has TEMPLATE.md been recently changed on main while the PR #3645 branch still has an older version?
Should the prepare job's 'git add -A' be scoped to only 'documentation/docfx/releases/' and 'scripts/infra/docs/pr-authors.json' to prevent capturing protected files?
workarounds: Add 'protected-files: fallback-to-issue' to the safe-outputs create-pull-request configuration in .github/workflows/update-release-notes.md to allow graceful degradation instead of hard failure.
Scope the 'git add' in the prepare job to only expected output directories: 'git add documentation/docfx/releases/ scripts/infra/docs/pr-authors.json' instead of 'git add -A'.

resolution.hypothesis: The prepare job uses 'git add -A' which is too broad — it captures protected files that are either present on the dispatched branch (for the first run) or appear as diffs relative to an older PR branch (for the main-branch runs). The fix is to either scope 'git add' to expected output paths, or configure 'protected-files: fallback-to-issue' as a safety net.

proposals.title: Scope git add to expected output directories
proposals.description: In the prepare job, replace 'git add -A' with 'git add -- documentation/docfx/releases/ scripts/infra/docs/pr-authors.json' so only the expected generated output is captured in the patch. This prevents any protected agent/workflow files from ever entering the prepare patch.

proposals.title: Add protected-files fallback configuration
proposals.description: Add 'protected-files: fallback-to-issue' to the safe-outputs create-pull-request section in update-release-notes.md. This converts hard failures into review issues instead of blocking the run, providing a safety net for any future cases where protected files unexpectedly appear.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Scope git add to expected output directories
resolution.recommendedReason: Fixing the root cause (overly broad 'git add -A') prevents protected files from ever entering the patch. The fallback option only masks the symptom. Both should be applied together for defense-in-depth.

actionability.reason: Root cause is likely the over-broad 'git add -A' in the prepare job, but the exact mechanism for main-branch runs including TEMPLATE.md needs confirmation before applying the fix. The fix requires modifying a protected workflow file.

actions.description: Post analysis identifying root cause and suggesting fix path
actions.comment:
**Root cause identified:** The prepare job in `.github/workflows/update-release-notes.md` uses `git add -A` which captures **all** working-tree changes — not just the expected documentation output under `documentation/docfx/releases/`. When the workflow runs from branch `mattleibow-release-notes-manual-notes` (which modifies `SKILL.md` and the compiled workflow YAML as part of PR #4334), those protected files end up in the prepare patch. The subsequent failures on main-branch runs (targeting PR #3645) suggest `SKILL.md` and `TEMPLATE.md` also appear in main-branch patches, possibly due to a drift between the PR #3645 branch head and current main.

**Recommended fix:** Replace `git add -A` in the prepare job with a scoped `git add -- documentation/docfx/releases/ scripts/infra/docs/pr-authors.json` to prevent protected files from ever entering the patch. As a safety net, also add `protected-files: fallback-to-issue` to the `safe-outputs.create-pull-request` configuration.

Similar issues: #4202, #4266 (both closed `not_planned` with the same pattern).
